name: Quercetin
effects: Shows antioxidant activity in lab models
contraindications: Long-term high-dose safety is not well established
Potential CYP/P-glycoprotein interactions are mostly theoretical
description: Non-essential phytochemical found in foods; supplement forms include aglycone and modified delivery products.
summary: Plant flavonoid supplement with limited clinical efficacy and bioavailability constraints.
mechanism: Proposed anti-inflammatory/mast-cell effects; translation of in vitro antioxidant findings to human outcomes remains uncertain.
safetyNotes: Generally safe at dietary intake levels.
Short-term studies around 500 mg/day show limited toxicity.
regulatoryStatus: Marketed as dietary supplement; no approved drug indication.
